title: 11 Password Registration and Client Centre Login Process
body_text:
11	Password Registration and Client Centre Login Process
11.1 	Overview of Password Registration and Client Centre Login Process
Client Centre Users and User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) log in to the Client Centre to register their user IDs and passwords. Security Officers are responsible for verifying the Client Centre UsersГўВҖВҷ identity before allowing them to proceed with registration.
Client Centre Users only require a 2-step security token to access an application, or functionality within an application, requiring 2-step authentication.
Important
:	Any changes made to a userГўВҖВҷs profile (e.g., application access) may take up to 45 minutes to come into effect on the Client Centre.
11.2	Client Centre Password Registration (All Users)
This section describes how to register a Client Centre password and applies to both Security Officers and Client Centre Users. Follow these procedures when logging in for the first time or after a password reset. Password registration is complete once the User ID is entered, and the one-time password is changed. User IDs and one-time passwords are obtained as follows.
Client User
ГўВҖВ“ Obtains the user ID and one-time password from their Security Officer.
Security Officer
ГўВҖВ“ Central
1 communicates the one-time password directly to the Security Officer. The Security OfficerГўВҖВҷs user ID is recorded on the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918.
Register your password as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and one-time password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Logging in to the Client Centre
Enter your one-time password in the old password field, then enter and confirm a new password. Select
Submit
. See Figure 2.
Note:	The new password must be a minimum of eight characters and can contain alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters. It canГўВҖВҷt be the same password used as one of the last six passwords.
Passwords are checked against a list of known unsecure passwords to prevent the use of passwords that are known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. This means that some password changes will fail even though the new password meets the structure requirements. If this occurs, the user should select a new password or use a passphrase instead, as these are harder to guess (e.g., ГўВҖВңdeskchairphonemugГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңI LOVE ice Cream dayГўВҖВқ).
Figure 2: Update Password
Related Information
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
11.3	Logging in to the Client Centre (All Users)
This section applies to Client Centre Users and Security Officers.
Log in to the Client Centre as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
.
Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Client Centre Login
To access your applications:
Select
Applications
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select the application that you want to access. See Figure 5.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 5: Select ГўВҖВңApplicationГўВҖВқ
Enter the code thatГўВҖВҷs generated on your registered 2-step security token (only if 2-step security authentication is required to access an application or a function within an application), then select
Done
. See Figure 6. See the 2-Step Security Part for procedures on installing and registering a 2-step security token.
Figure 6: Enter Code from the 2-Step Security Token
END
